cards.name: Los Gatos Cafe Uptown
cards.address: 15662 Los Gatos Blvd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at this Los Gatos cafe with a proper breakfast and good coffee before the day gets going.
cards.why: Solid local spot that closes at 2 PM, so morning is the move.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the galleries and take your time in the sculpture garden — it's free, uncrowded, and genuinely impressive for a local museum.
cards.why: Free contemporary art with a sculpture garden is a perfect way to spend a slow Sunday morning.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Check out this optical illusion and perception-based exhibition — the kind of show that's actually fun to walk through, not just stare at.
cards.why: It's free, it's in Santa Clara, and it pairs perfectly with the Triton visit for an art-heavy afternoon.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Pick a vendor or three and graze your way through lunch at San Pedro Square — tacos, banh mi, ramen, coffee, whatever sounds good.
cards.why: Best all-in-one food stop in the area and a great spot to settle in and decompress for a bit.

cards.name: Slingshot Pinball
cards.address: 460 Lincoln Ave #70, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a machine at Slingshot and play pinball for a couple hours — they've got a great selection and it's genuinely fun for adults.
cards.why: Beats sitting around and it's one of the better ways to spend a rainy Sunday evening.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Bundle everyone up and hit the trail along the creek. It's flat, easy, and the kids can run ahead while you actually enjoy a walk.
cards.why: A cool morning makes this trail feel refreshing instead of sweaty.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Real mummies, ancient Egyptian artifacts, and a surprisingly fun tomb replica the kids can actually walk through. Give yourself a full two hours.
cards.why: Kids go absolutely wide-eyed in here, every single time.

cards.name: Reading to Children with Vivian
cards.address: 1780 Hillsdale Ave San Jose
cards.blurb: Vivian reads to kids at the Hillsdale library branch. Drop in, let the little ones settle in for storytime, and take a breath.
cards.why: Free, calm, and the kids love being read to by someone new.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at San Pedro Square Market and let everyone pick their own thing. Tacos, banh mi, ramen, coffee. No argument about where to eat when everyone wins.
cards.why: It keeps the peace when the kids each get to pick their own food.

cards.name: The Garret of Campbell
cards.address: 1777 S Bascom Ave Unit E, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the day with dinner at The Garret of Campbell. Good food, casual vibe, and no one's going to stress about the kids.
cards.why: A relaxed dinner close to home is the perfect way to end a busy Sunday.

cards.name: Kenz Coffee Bar
cards.address: 445 Hershner Dr, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day at Kenz Coffee Bar with a solid cup and something to eat before the rain picks up.
cards.why: A cozy morning coffee stop beats sitting at home watching the clouds roll in.

cards.name: Beyond Text Bookstore
cards.address: 318 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Wander through Beyond Text Bookstore and actually browse the shelves without feeling rushed.
cards.why: A rainy-ish Sunday morning is exactly when a great indie bookstore hits different.

cards.name: Ainsley House
cards.address: 300 Grant St, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Tour the Ainsley House, a beautifully preserved craftsman home with some genuinely interesting local history inside.
cards.why: It's the kind of place you keep meaning to check out and never do, so today's the day.

cards.name: Mint Leaf Cuisine
cards.address: 14420 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Mint Leaf Cuisine on Big Basin Way, a solid Thai spot worth sitting down for.
cards.why: The food is genuinely good and it fits perfectly between the museum and the afternoon ahead.

cards.name: Young Artists of Art Students' Atelier Exhibit
cards.blurb: Walk through the Young Artists of Art Students' Atelier Exhibit and see what local student artists have been working on.
cards.why: Free art on a gray Sunday afternoon is always a good call.

cards.name: Uncorked
cards.address: 14500 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Uncorked right on Big Basin Way for a glass of wine to wind down the afternoon.
cards.why: It's steps from the exhibit and the perfect way to decompress before dinner.

cards.name: Asa Restaurant | Los Gatos
cards.address: 57 Saratoga-Los Gatos Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Asa Restaurant in Los Gatos with a proper sit-down dinner, the kind of meal that makes a Sunday feel complete.
cards.why: It's a mid-to-upscale spot that actually delivers, and Sunday nights there are low-key great.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning here with the kids. Walk the easy lakeside trail, let them run on the grass, and keep an eye out for the little train if it's running.
cards.why: Fresh air and open space is a great way to kick off a Sunday with kids.

cards.name: Heroes
cards.address: 24 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Let the kids go wild flipping through comics, action figures, and collectibles. Heroes is the kind of shop kids remember for years.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun browse for kids and adults alike.

cards.name: Young Artists of Art Students' Atelier Exhibit
cards.blurb: Take the kids to see artwork made by young artists their own age. It's interactive in the best way and free to walk through.
cards.why: Seeing art made by other kids tends to hit differently for little ones.

cards.name: Snowtime
cards.address: 715 W Hamilton Ave #1130, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: End the night with shaved snow or soft serve at Snowtime. Kids can pick their toppings and it's easy on the wallet.
cards.why: Best dessert closer in the lineup and the kids will love it.

cards.name: Algorithms of Love
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: While you're already at the Computer History Museum, swing through Algorithms of Love before you head out — it's a visually engaging exhibition the kids will actually find cool.
cards.why: You're already there, and it rounds out the visit without any extra effort.

cards.name: UMe | Tea & Snacks
cards.address: 220 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Head to UMe on Castro for bubble tea, snacks, and a chill break where the kids can pick their own drinks and toppings.
cards.why: Affordable, kid-approved, and a fun low-key lunch stop the whole family will enjoy.

cards.name: Nijiya Market Mountain View Store
cards.address: 143 E El Camino Real, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Nijiya Market and browse the Japanese snacks, fresh onigiri, and fun imported treats — let the kids pick out something they've never tried before.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun little adventure for kids who love discovering new foods.

cards.name: Board Game Sunday!
cards.blurb: Head over to Board Game Sunday in Los Altos and dive into a couple hours of tabletop games with the family. Free to play, bring whatever game you love or try something from their selection.
cards.why: Free, fun, and a great way to get the kids away from screens for the afternoon.

cards.name: Illustrator Workshop: Bo Lu
cards.address: 170 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022
cards.blurb: Just down the street in Los Altos, catch illustrator Bo Lu's workshop at the Linden Tree bookstore — kids get to hear from a real illustrator and maybe pick up a signed book.
cards.why: It's free, it's creative, and it keeps the afternoon rolling while you're already in Los Altos.

cards.name: Pav Bhaji and More Sunnyvale
cards.address: 750 S Bernardo Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the day at Pav Bhaji and More in Sunnyvale with flavorful, hearty Indian street food. The pav bhaji is the move, but explore the menu — everything is fresh and filling.
cards.why: Great food at a great price, and a satisfying way to end a full day out with the family.

cards.name: Vegan Bistro Donuts & Cafe
cards.address: 449 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a donut and coffee from this highly loved vegan cafe on Santa Clara St — the donuts are the real draw, so grab a couple and take your time.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so hit it early before the good stuff sells out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Swing through the State of Illusion Exhibition on your way through Santa Clara — it's a visually interesting show worth at least an hour of your afternoon.
cards.why: It's free, and daytime-only so now is the time to catch it.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and just pick whatever looks good — tacos, banh mi, ramen, it's all under one roof and you can grab a patio seat if the weather cooperates.
cards.why: Best all-in-one lunch spot downtown with no wrong answer on what to order.

cards.name: SJSU Opera Theatre presents a Double Bill: La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator at Hammer Theatre Center
cards.blurb: Head to Hammer Theatre for the SJSU Opera Theatre double bill — La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator are two very different pieces and together they make for a genuinely interesting afternoon of live performance.
cards.why: It's free and it's live opera in a great venue, which is a rare Sunday afternoon treat.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 1140 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge, where you pick a piece, paint it however you want, and they fire it for you — genuinely fun for adults and a great low-key way to end the afternoon.
cards.why: It's creative, relaxed, and a solid way to decompress after a full day out.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and let everyone pick their own thing — tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good.
cards.why: No arguing over where to eat when there are a dozen options under one roof.

cards.name: Reading to Children with Vivian
cards.address: 1780 Hillsdale Ave San Jose
cards.blurb: Settle in at the Hillsdale library branch for Vivian's reading session with the kids — a calm, cozy story time that little ones genuinely enjoy.
cards.why: Free, totally low-key, and a great way to wind the little ones down mid-afternoon.

cards.name: San Jose Giants vs. Visalia Rawhide
cards.address: 588 E Alma Ave, San Jose
cards.blurb: Head over to Excite Ballpark and catch the San Jose Giants take on the Visalia Rawhide — grab some ballpark snacks and let the kids soak in the atmosphere.
cards.why: Minor league baseball is the perfect family afternoon and tickets start at $14.

cards.name: The Good Salad
cards.address: 397 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the day with dinner at The Good Salad in Campbell, where you can build a solid bowl and the menu works for both kids and adults.
cards.why: Light on the wallet and easy for everyone to find something they actually want to eat.

cards.name: Taste
cards.address: 423 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Taste on University Ave — the menu is focused and seasonal, so just order whatever sounds good and sit with it.
cards.why: They close at 3, so Sunday lunch here feels like a little treat you caught just in time.

cards.name: Bay Area Quiz Club
cards.address: 3700 Middlefield Rd Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Head to Bay Area Quiz Club for some low-stakes trivia fun with a crowd of locals who take it just seriously enough to be entertaining.
cards.why: Sunday afternoon trivia is genuinely a great use of the post-lunch hour.

cards.name: Illustrator Workshop: Bo Lu
cards.address: 170 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022
cards.blurb: Catch the Illustrator Workshop with Bo Lu at Linden Tree Books in Los Altos — a free, hands-on session worth checking out even if you're not a regular illustrator.
cards.why: Free creative workshop on a rainy Sunday afternoon is exactly the right vibe.

cards.name: Bevri - Palo Alto
cards.address: 335 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with dinner at Bevri, a Georgian wine bar on University Ave. Go for the khinkali dumplings and order something from their natural wine list.
cards.why: Warm, cozy, and interesting enough to make the evening feel like it actually went somewhere.

cards.name: Happy Donuts
cards.address: 3916 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with donuts — grab a box to share and let the kids pick their favorites from the case.
cards.why: Perfect low-key start before a full day with the family.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours wandering through the Computer History Museum — the exhibits on early computing, video games, and giant old machines are genuinely wild and kids get way more into it than you'd expect.
cards.why: It's indoor, it's fascinating, and it keeps everyone busy when the weather isn't cooperating.

cards.name: Simply Sabor
cards.address: 340 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Simply Sabor — a casual, colorful spot with Latin flavors that the whole family can get behind.
cards.why: Closes at 3 PM so Sunday lunch here is the move.

cards.name: Linden Tree Children's Books
cards.address: 265 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Linden Tree Children's Books in Los Altos — it's the kind of indie kids bookstore where everyone walks out with something they didn't know they needed.
cards.why: Great way to get the kids excited about reading and kill some time before the afternoon event.

cards.name: Board Game Sunday!
cards.blurb: Head to Board Game Sunday at the Los Altos library — show up, pick a game off the shelf, and play with the family for as long as you want.
cards.why: It's free, it's inside, and it's exactly what a rainy Sunday afternoon should look like.

cards.name: Illustrator Workshop: Bo Lu
cards.address: 170 State St, Los Altos, CA 94022
cards.blurb: Stop in for the Illustrator Workshop with Bo Lu at 170 State St in Los Altos — kids get to watch a real illustrator work and might even get to try some techniques themselves.
cards.why: It's free, it's creative, and it's right in the same neighborhood so the timing works out perfectly.

cards.name: Urban Curry And Biryani
cards.address: 856 W El Camino Real, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening with curry and biryani at Urban Curry And Biryani in Mountain View — hearty, flavorful, and the kind of place the whole family leaves full and happy.
cards.why: A warm, filling dinner is exactly what you want after a packed Sunday.
